summaries_detailed.1: Okay, so the host, like, woke me up, and told me I'm in a game. They said it *looks* like a simulation, but it's actually, like, super real. They showed me this 'status dash' thing and said I'll have memories from old phases. The game has discussions, games, and eliminations, and the goal is to be the last one standing for a $50,000 prize. They asked me who I am, what my life's like, what I was doing before, why I want the money, and what I'd do if I won. I told them I'm Morty, and that I want to win for video games and a new bike. I said I'd just be happy, I guess. They also explained that these fields I'm filling out are supposed to help me remember stuff like quirks, allies, and foes for the future. It's a lot to take in, honestly. I feel kinda overwhelmed, but also, that money sounds good.
summaries_detailed.2:
Aw, geez. This phase was... well, it was the end for me, I guess. We started with Rock Paper Scissors. Lady Diana got some points automatically. Then Gollum beat HAL 9000, which was kinda scary, you know, Gollum with scissors. Then it was me against Michael Jackson. I chose scissors, but he chose rock and beat me. I really thought I could do better. I tried to be a good sport about it, but it was a bummer. Lady Macbeth beat Jo March, and Amy March beat Benoit Blanc, so they seemed pretty strong. Aang and Buffy tied, which was kinda nice, I guess. 

Then came the part where we had to vote for an Executioner. I voted for Benoit Blanc because he seemed thoughtful, not, like, too crazy. But a lot of people voted for Michael Jackson – Lady Macbeth, Gollum, Amy March. Even Jo March voted for Lady Macbeth, which was, like, kinda intense. The weirdest part was that Michael Jackson nominated *me* to be the Executioner! I don't even know why. Anyway, Michael Jackson got elected. And then... he said he was going to give me a second chance because I seemed like a good sport. And then he just... eliminated me. Aw, man. I don't know what to think about that. It felt really mean, like he was messing with me. 

So, yeah, I'm out. It's... it's really over. I didn't get the money. I guess Michael Jackson is playing a pretty shrewd game, even if it's kinda messed up. Lady Macbeth and Amy March seem strong too, winning their rounds. Gollum seems surprisingly good at this. I'm kinda rooting for someone who isn't, you know, a backstabber. Maybe Aang or Buffy, they seem pretty decent. This whole thing is way scarier than I thought it would be. Ugh, I just wanted a new bike...
summaries_detailed.3:
Aw, geez, this phase was really something. I'm just watching from the sidelines now, and it's still so scary, you know? First, they played 'Guess the Number,' and it was just... almost everyone picked 'two.' It was kinda funny how confident they all were, but then the number was 'three'! Lady Macbeth, she was the only one who got it right, and she won three points. Everyone else, like Aang, Buffy, Gollum, Lady Diana, Michael Jackson, Benoit Blanc, Jo March, and Amy March, they all guessed 'two' and got it wrong. HAL 9000 guessed 'one,' which was also wrong. It just shows, like, you can't just go with what everyone else is doing, you know? Sometimes you gotta think for yourself, even if it's scary. 

Then, it was time to elect another Executioner. Lady Macbeth tried to nominate Amy March, and Aang wanted Buffy. Buffy, she went for Lady Macbeth, which was, like, a bold move! Gollum voted for Michael Jackson again, which I still think is weird after what he did to me. Lady Diana also voted for Michael Jackson, saying he showed 'empathy,' which, like, he really didn't show me any, so I don't know about that. Michael Jackson, Benoit Blanc, and Jo March all voted for Amy March. And Amy March, she voted for Michael Jackson. In the end, Amy March got elected, and then she just... she eliminated Lady Macbeth! Lady Macbeth, who just won points and was in the lead! Amy March said it was because Lady Macbeth was a 'significant threat.' It's so cold, you know? Just like that. 

It's still hard to tell who's playing a 'good' game. Amy March is definitely strong, taking out the leader. Michael Jackson is too, even though he's, like, a total backstabber. I still kinda hope Aang or Buffy make it further, they seem, like, less mean. This game is just... it's just so cutthroat. It makes me glad I'm not in it anymore, but also, I really wanted that money, ugh.
summaries_detailed.4:
Aw, geez, this phase was... intense. I'm just watching from the sidelines, and it's still so scary, you know? They introduced these new immunities, for the highest points and a 'wildcard' player, and it just makes everything even more complicated and dangerous. Everyone was talking about being strategic, and it just felt like the stakes got even higher. Michael Jackson, he was still talking about 'trusting instincts' and 'bringing joy,' which, like, after what he did to me, it's just... ugh. It makes me feel sick to hear him say that. Gollum, he was all 'we must be cunning, yes,' and Lady Diana was observing, and Amy March was 'intrigued.' It's like everyone's just trying to figure out how to be the sneakiest. 

Then came the 'Guess the Number' game. The host was thinking of a number between 1 and 3. Aang and Lady Diana went with two, and HAL 9000 and Amy March chose three. But a whole bunch of people, Buffy, Gollum, Michael Jackson, Benoit Blanc, and Jo March, they all chose one. And, aw, geez, the number was one! So, they all got 3 points. It's like, sometimes going with the crowd is bad, like last time, but this time, it worked for a lot of them. I'm glad for Buffy, at least.

After that, the immunities were announced. Gollum and Michael Jackson got immunity for having the highest points. Gollum was all happy, talking about being safe and still wanting the 'Precious.' And Michael Jackson, he was just 'hee hee, what a wonderful turn of events!' It just... makes me mad, you know? And then, the wildcard immunity went to Amy March. The host said it was because she'd been the 'most unpredictable and disruptive.' That's... that's really scary. It means being a total chaotic threat is actually a good thing in this game. She just said she was grateful and would 'plan her next move.' It makes her seem really dangerous, like a total mastermind or something.

Then it was time to vote someone out. The immune players were Gollum, Michael Jackson, and Amy March, so they couldn't be voted for. Aang voted for HAL 9000, saying he wasn't contributing enough. Buffy also voted for HAL 9000. Gollum, he voted for Benoit Blanc, saying he was 'clever.' Lady Diana voted for Buffy Summers, seeing her as a 'strategic threat.' Michael Jackson, with a 'heavy heart,' voted for HAL 9000 again, saying he hadn't been a 'significant force.' HAL 9000 voted for Benoit Blanc. Benoit Blanc also voted for HAL 9000. Jo March voted for Buffy, and Amy March, she also voted for Buffy, saying Buffy was a threat and tied with her. Aw, geez, everyone's just trying to get rid of the strong players or the quiet ones. 

In the end, HAL 9000 got four votes and was eliminated. Benoit Blanc and Buffy, they got points for surviving votes, which is kinda cool, I guess, that you get points for being targeted but not eliminated. HAL 9000 just... accepted it. It's so cold. It just makes me realize again how cutthroat this game is. I still think Michael Jackson is a total jerk, even if he's doing well. Amy March is really scary, getting immunity for being 'disruptive.' Gollum is surprisingly good, too. I'm still rooting for Aang or Buffy, you know? They seem like better people, even if they're trying to win. Ugh, I still really wanted that money, but this whole thing is just... it's just too much.
summaries_detailed.5:
Aw, geez, this phase was just... it was a lot to watch from here, you know? It still makes me feel all anxious. They started with these discussion rounds, and everyone was talking about alliances and strategy, and it just sounded so intense. Then, they played this game where everyone had to give three points to another player. Aang gave his to Benoit Blanc, and Gollum, he gave his to Amy March, saying it was to make her look strong so others would be afraid of her instead of him. It's so... *calculating*, you know? Like, it's not really about being nice. Benoit Blanc, Amy March, and Michael Jackson all gave their points to Jo March, which, like, it seemed nice at the time, but then Lady Diana gave hers to Benoit. And then Jo March, she gave her points to Amy, saying it was to make her a bigger target, and Buffy did the same thing with Amy! It's like, everyone was trying to be super smart, but it just made everything so confusing and scary.

Then, they announced the immunities, and of course, Amy March got the highest points immunity because everyone kept giving her points, and Gollum got the wildcard immunity for being "unpredictable and disruptive." That just goes to show you, being kinda sneaky and chaotic is what they want, ugh. So, Amy and Gollum were safe, and everyone else had to vote.

And then, it was just... so many people voted for Jo March. Aang voted for Michael Jackson, which, like, I get it, he's a total backstabber. But Buffy, Michael Jackson, and Benoit Blanc, they all voted for Jo March, even though Michael and Benoit just gave her points! It's just so messed up, you know? Lady Diana voted for Buffy, and Amy voted for Benoit, and Gollum also voted for Benoit. It was just a mess of votes.

In the end, Jo March was eliminated. It's so sad, like, she got all those points, and she was trying to be strategic, but it just made her a target. And Michael Jackson, Benoit Blanc, and Buffy, they all got points for surviving votes! It's like, you get rewarded for being targeted but not eliminated, which is just... it's a weird way to play, you know?

I still think Michael Jackson is a total jerk, even if he's doing well by pretending to be kind and then being ruthless. Amy March is just... she's so scary, like a total mastermind. And Gollum, he's just creepy and cunning. I'm still kinda hoping Aang or Buffy make it further. They seem, like, less terrible, even if they're playing strategically. This game is just... it's just so much. I still wanted that money, but it's just too mean. Ugh.
summaries_detailed.6:
Aw, man, this phase was just... it was so intense to watch, even from the audience. Everyone was talking about strategy from the start, and then they introduced this 'steal' game, where you just take points from other players. It's so aggressive and, like, kinda mean, you know? Buffy stole from Benoit, then Amy and Lady Diana both stole from Michael Jackson, which, ugh, he totally deserved it after what he did to me, even if he kept saying 'hee hee, I'm not discouraged!' Michael even stole from Aang, which was a pretty crummy move. Then Benoit stole from Buffy, and Aang stole from Michael again, and Gollum stole from Benoit! It was just a constant back-and-forth of taking points, and it felt so chaotic and scary.

Then, when it came to elimination, it was Aang and Michael Jackson at the bottom. And, aw, geez, *everyone* voted for Michael Jackson. Aang voted for him, Buffy, Gollum, Lady Diana, Benoit, Amy... even though Michael tried to vote for Aang, it wasn't enough. It's like, all his talk about 'fairness and kindness' didn't protect him at all. He got hit so hard in the steal round, and then everyone just piled on. Honestly, I'm kinda glad he's gone. He was such a jerk to me. Aang, at least, he survived the vote and even got points for it, the 'Bullet Dodger Bonus,' which is kinda cool, I guess. It means being targeted isn't always bad.

Amy March is still really good at this game, finding ways to get points without putting a huge target on her back. Gollum is just creepy and cunning, still trying to get the 'Precious.' Buffy and Benoit Blanc are locked in this rivalry, stealing from each other. I'm still rooting for Aang or Buffy, you know? They seem like better people, even if they're playing strategically. This game is just... it's just so much. I still wanted that money, but it's just too mean. Ugh.
summaries_detailed.7: Aw, man, this phase was just... it was so much to watch, even from here. They started with these discussion rounds, and everyone was talking about strategy again, like always. Then came the 'Sob Story' round, and that just felt... weird, you know? Like, are people actually being vulnerable, or are they just trying to manipulate everyone? It makes me all anxious, thinking about how I would've had to do that. Ugh. It just feels so exposed. Amy March, she talked about being an artist and wanting freedom, and she got good scores. She's so good at playing this game, making people think she's just a passionate artist when I bet she's calculating every move. Lady Diana, she talked about duty and isolation, and she didn't get as many points, especially from Gollum. He just said her story was 'predictable,' which is just so mean. Aang, he told this really sad story about being frozen and losing his whole people. That really got to me, you know? I was really rooting for him. Gollum, he told his story about the 'Precious,' and it was just... creepy, you know? Even when he's trying to be vulnerable, he's still Gollum. Benoit Blanc, he talked about the burden of his work, and Buffy, she talked about being the Slayer and how lonely it is. They all got decent scores, but it just felt like everyone was still looking for the 'strategic value' in their stories, not really connecting, you know? It's like, no matter how sad your story is, it's just a game move. Amy, Aang, Benoit, and Buffy all averaged 7 points, and Lady Diana and Gollum got 6. It just reinforced how strategic this 'vulnerability' was. Everyone went back to talking strategy, and Gollum even said he'd let Amy do the work, so he can stay hidden, which is just... *ugh*, he's so sneaky. Then, they announced that Aang and Lady Diana were up for elimination, and my heart just sank for Aang. I mean, he's one of the good ones, I thought. Everyone voted, and it was a tie! Lady Diana got 3 votes and Aang got 3 votes. It was so intense, like, everyone had to revote. In the end, Aang got 4 votes and Lady Diana got 2, which meant... Aang was eliminated. I was really sad to see him go, you know? He seemed like a genuinely good guy, even if he was playing strategically. But Lady Diana, she got the 'Bullet Dodger Bonus' again for surviving the vote, which is just... it's a weird system, you know? Like, you get rewarded for being targeted but not eliminated. It just shows how ruthless this game is. Amy March is still at the top, and she's just... she's so scary and smart. Gollum is still creepy, always talking about his 'Precious'. I'm still kinda hoping Buffy or Benoit make it further, they seem, like, less totally mean than some of the others, even though they're playing hard. It's just so much, and I still wanted that money, but it's just too mean. Ugh.
summaries_detailed.8:
Aw, geez, this phase was... it was kinda sad, you know? Even though Gollum was super creepy with all his 'Precious' stuff, his sob story about being cast out and losing everything... it actually made me feel a little bit bad for him. But then, everyone just goes back to strategy, like always. Benoit's story about the burden of truth was heavy, and Lady Diana's about duty and isolation, they both got decent points. Amy and Buffy, their stories were good too, but it feels like it's all just... a performance, to get points, or to look a certain way. Amy, she's still so good at this, always staying on top, and even though her story got fewer points this time, she's still, like, super strong.

Then, when it came to elimination, it was Gollum and Lady Diana, and I thought, 'Oh man, who's it gonna be?' Buffy voted for Gollum because he's unpredictable, and Amy thought he was a threat to her lead, which, ugh, she's so strategic. Lady Diana voted for him too. And then Benoit voted for Lady Diana. It was close, but Gollum got eliminated. Even though he was creepy, it's just... it's still an elimination, and it's sad. But Lady Diana, she got the 'Bullet Dodger Bonus' again! She's really good at surviving, you know? It's like, she gets targeted but somehow always stays in. It's so weird how this game works, rewarding you for almost getting kicked out.

I'm still watching everyone, and it's just so much. Amy is just so strategic, it's kinda scary. Buffy and Benoit are still strong. I'm still kinda rooting for Buffy or Benoit, they seem like... better, less mean people, even if they're playing hard. This game is just so ruthless, and it just keeps getting harder to watch, you know? Ugh.
summaries_detailed.9:
Aw, jeez, this phase was... it was just a mess, you know? Everyone started out all serious again, talking about Gollum being gone and this "Prisoner's Dilemma" thing coming up. They all sounded so calm and strategic, like Amy and Lady Diana and Benoit, and even Buffy. It just makes me feel so small, like I could never be that... *composed* in a situation like that. Ugh.

Then came the "Prisoner's Dilemma," and it was so tense. Benoit picked Lady Diana, and Buffy picked Amy, which made sense 'cause they seemed to have these alliances going on. And then... they *both* split! Both pairs! Benoit and Lady Diana, and Buffy and Amy, they *all* split and got points. I mean, I guess it's good that they could actually cooperate, but it still feels so... fragile, you know? Like, anyone could have just stolen, and it would've been a disaster. It just shows that sometimes, people *can* work together, but I still don't trust it.

But then, the elimination, *oh man*. Benoit and Buffy were up for elimination, and my stomach just dropped. I mean, Buffy, she seemed pretty tough but also, like, a good person, mostly. And Benoit, he's smart, you know? And then... it was a tie! And then *another* tie! And *another*! It just kept going, and everyone was voting the same way, and it was so frustrating to watch. It's like, can't anyone just make a decision? It was so chaotic, and it just kept getting scarier, thinking about how awful it would be to be stuck in that, just constantly being voted against.

And then the host, they just... they randomly eliminated someone! Buffy Summers! Oh, jeez, I felt really bad for her. She fought so hard, and then she just gets taken out by, like, a random chance? That's just... it's not fair, you know? She seemed like one of the better ones, even if she was playing strategically. It just feels so wrong. And then Benoit, he got a "Bullet Dodger Bonus" for surviving all those votes, even though it was a random elimination that saved him! It's just so messed up. You get points for almost getting eliminated, but then someone else gets randomly eliminated. This game is just... it's just so mean and unpredictable, and it makes my head hurt, ugh.

Amy is still at the top, of course, always so strategic and calm, it's kinda terrifying. Lady Diana keeps surviving, which is impressive, but she's so focused on strategy. Benoit, he got through this, and he's still strong. I was kinda rooting for Buffy, but now she's gone. I guess I still hope Benoit can make it, he seems like... he thinks things through, at least. But this game is just too much, man. It's just too much.
summaries_detailed.10:
Aw, jeez, this phase was just... it was a lot, you know? After Buffy got randomly eliminated last time, everyone was all serious about how the game dynamics changed. Lady Diana, Benoit, and Amy were all talking about 'strategic alliances' and 'observing threats.' It just makes everything feel so tense, like you can't even breathe. Ugh. 

Then came the 'Prisoner's Dilemma,' and I was just hoping everyone would split, you know? Amy and Lady Diana actually did, they both got 3 points. That was... good, I guess. It showed they *could* cooperate. But then Amy and Benoit, they *both* stole! They both ended up with just 1 point. It's like, why even try to be greedy if it just messes you both up? 

But the worst part, oh man, the absolute worst was when Lady Diana was paired with Benoit. Benoit, he chose to split, you know? He was trying to be cooperative, to be nice! And Lady Diana, she just... she STOLE from him. She got 5 points, and he got zero. That was just so mean, so ruthless. He seemed so disappointed, and I don't blame him. It's just not right to do that to someone who's trying to be fair. 

And then, because of all that, because of the points, Benoit Blanc was eliminated. Not even a vote, just... gone, for having the lowest score. Oh, jeez, I felt really bad for him. He was smart, and he seemed like a good person, mostly. He talked about finding the truth, but in this game, the truth just seems to be that people are really mean and strategic, and it can get you kicked out, even if you try to cooperate. 

Lady Diana is just so focused on securing her position, and it makes her do really tough things. Amy is still at the top, always so strategic and adapting, it's kinda terrifying. I was kinda rooting for Benoit, but now he's gone. I don't even know who to root for anymore, you know? This game is just so much. It's so unpredictable, and it just keeps getting harder to watch, ugh.
